title: Nephrodium gracilescens
taxonomy: Nephrodium gracilescens belongs to the family Polypodiaceae, which is a part of the larger group of ferns known as Polypodiales. This species is a type of fern, classified under the division Pteridophyta. Within the class Polypodiopsida, it is specifically classified as an order Polypodiales. At the family level, it is categorized under Polypodiaceae, a family of ferns commonly known as polypods. It is further classified under the genus Nephrodium, which is now considered to be a synonym for the genus Polypodium.
uses: Nephrodium gracilescens is not a commonly recognized species, but I can tell you about related ferns. However, some ferns in the Nephrolepis genus have been used for their medicinal properties.
